title: Darth Who Contest
content:
{{Top|real}}
{{Citation}}
[[File:Darthwho-tall.jpg|thumb]]
The winning entry in the "'''Darth Who Contest'''" was the official [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] name of [[Darth Caedus|a major character]] revealed in the book ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'', the fifth in the [[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]] series. The contest ended at 12:00 am EST on [[December 1]], [[2006]]; according to the [[February]]/[[March]] [[2007]] [[Del Rey]] ''[[Star Wars]]'' newsletter, 8500 entries were received. The top five names were chosen by the editors at Lucasfilm and Del Rey, and the authors of the Legacy of the Force series. Their selections were announced on [[January 23]], 2007.

According to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', Jacen's placeholder Sith name under the Darth Who Contest was Darth Kallus.

==Rules==
The contest was open to all registered members of the Star Wars [[StarWars.com|official site]] who were American citizens over the age of 18. Each member was able to submit one name only. The contest lasted from [[October 3]] to [[November 30]], 2006. [[Aaron Allston]], [[Troy Denning]], and [[Karen Traviss]] selected the top five entries, which were then posted on the official site on January 23 and remained on the site for voting until [[February 27]], 2007. During that time, registered members of the official site could vote for the winner.

==Implications==
It had been originally thought that the Legacy book series might determine whether or not [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] would become [[Darth Krayt]] of the [[Star Wars: Legacy|''Legacy'']] comics (a common fan theory at the time before the book was released), but the announcement of the contest altered this concept, as the Sith name was determined by contest and Darth Krayt was not one of the potential names. The identity of Darth Krayt was later revealed in a ''Legacy'' comic as the former Jedi Master A'Sharad Hett.

==Candidates==
{|{{Prettytable}}
!Name !! Author !! Actual meaning !! Official LFL description
|-
| '''Darth Acheron'''|| "ShadowFox41402"|| [[Wikipedia:Acheron|Acheron]] is the river over which [[Wikipedia:Charon (mythology)|Charon]] ferries the dead to [[Wikipedia:Hades|Hades]]|| A river in [[Chaos/Legends|Hell]] for those who deny justice
|-
| '''[[Darth Caedus#Darth Caedus is born|Darth Caedus]]'''|| "[[Tawnia Poland]]"|| Variant of ''caedes'' which, in [[Wikipedia:Latin|Latin]], means cutting down, killing, slaughter or referring to persons slain; blood shed in slaughter|| A battle of darkness
|-
| '''Darth Judicar'''|||| Possible variant of "[[Wikipedia:Adjudicator|adjudicator]]." Can be translated from Latin as Justice.|| The final judge
|-
| '''Darth Paxis'''|| "Jedi_Master_Monkey"|| Variant of [[Wikipedia:Pax|pax]]|| Bringer of peace
|-
| '''Darth Taral'''|| "katsqueak"|| Variant of [[Wikipedia:tare|tare]]: "in chemistry, an empty vessel similar to one being used in an experiment and placed beside it to show or counter balance any change in its weight"|| Ancient [[Sith (language)|Sith]] for "he who protects"
|-
|}

==Winner==
The winner, as announced in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'', was [[Darth Caedus]]. In the acknowledgements in the beginning of the novel, Del Rey Books and Lucas Licencing Ltd. thanked the winner, [[Tawnia Poland]], for contributing the name. There was also a thank you to all the fans who took part in the Darth Who contest.
